nodes.label: ideas1.md (origin blueprint)
nodes.rationale: The nine-chapter day-one derivation of draft 6.0 (two brains, mono-forward, LUT, plasticity gradient, gate, sleep, boosting) + the consolidated math + the experiment ladder; written before Phase 1.

nodes.label: Two brains (80/20 SCFF + GD)
nodes.rationale: Cheap ~80% unsupervised SCFF organizes the world for free; small precise ~20% (GD-role) puts our labels on it. SCFF = senses+cortex sorting into kinds; the namer puts names on. The whole reason the chip can be cheap.

nodes.label: Direction is the expensive part of learning
nodes.rationale: The one law surviving the draft-5 fire: magnitude is cheap (substrate measures |a.W| free), direction costs a backward pass/transpose/chain. So pay for direction ONCE, in one place, make everything else cheap.

nodes.label: SCFF (Self-Contrastive Forward-Forward)
nodes.rationale: Label-free, forward-only, local, derivative-free learner: two forward passes (positive x_k+x_k, negative x_k+x_n) trained so goodness ||h||^2 is high on positives/low on negatives. The only rule that is local AND derivative-free AND forward-only AND unsupervised at once.

nodes.label: Goodness (sum ||h||^2)
nodes.rationale: The per-layer energy of activations; deliberately the SUM ||h||^2 not the paper's mean (sum is ~1 at init, width-independent, so theta=2.0 works under plain online SGD; the mean form starves deep layers to ~1/M).

nodes.label: Mandatory inter-layer normalization
nodes.rationale: h_hat = h/||h|| between layers; required by FF so the next layer can't recycle the previous goodness; load-bearing twice - also the guard against the residual shortcut (inflating ||h||^2 by passing input through).

nodes.label: LUT prototype memory (hippocampus stand-in)
nodes.rationale: A deduplicated winner-take-all store of raw-input prototypes (novelty allocation at vigilance tau). One store, three customers: SCFF negatives, sleep replay, seed of the future memory model. Store raw inputs not features (features drift). A service, not a parallel brain.

nodes.label: The precise brain / GD namer (~20%)
nodes.rationale: Maps SCFF's clean unsupervised clusters onto real labels; the only place direction gets paid for. Planned as GD; Phase 7 (S11) retired GD entirely -> the namer is closed-form (RanPAC/SLDA).

nodes.label: Plasticity gradient (drift fix, mirror LLRD)
nodes.rationale: The read-layers GD taps learn slow, front layers stay fast (eta_l = eta_0 * rho^d(l)); bounds SCFF drift for free (weaker coupling on those Scaps). The budget-correct fix: slow the read-layers (free) rather than fire GD more (burns the 80/20). Struck in P9 (N2).

nodes.label: Threshold-gated learning (epsilon-floor)
nodes.rationale: Loss < theta -> SCFF only (cheap local); Loss >= theta -> SCFF + GD. Justified by the gradient-mismatch epsilon-floor (local learning stalls when block disagreement dominates). Refinement: gate on loss-slope (plateau). P8 resolved it to a DDM gate on the namer's error-EMA - a SAFETY mechanism (firing more forgets more).

nodes.label: Sleep consolidation
nodes.rationale: Periodic full-batch GD over LUT history against the CURRENT SCFF map, re-covering the whole range the gate under-covers; cheap because the SCFF body is frozen and the namer is ~20%. Frozen in P9 at grid-4 cadence.

nodes.label: Residual boosting blocks (BoostResNet)
nodes.rationale: Chain [SCFF -> middle -> GD] units as a residual stream where each block is a weak corrector of the residual its predecessors left; training error falls fast with depth; credit stays mostly local. Dropped in the committed cell (single frozen bulk + reading namer, S11 / P2.5 forward-leak wall).

nodes.label: GD reads via taps (read, never write SCFF)
nodes.rationale: The namer taps the last n SCFF layers and only reads them, so SCFF and GD never share a weight (dissolves the contested-weight drift problem) and gives a free stop-gradient (BYOL anti-collapse). P2.5: write-back kills SCFF.

nodes.label: Two GD organs (Interface GD / Output GD)
nodes.rationale: Interface GD (small, per-block exit, tracking, SGD+momentum, squared error) vs Output GD (final brain, precision, Adam-class online / full-batch at sleep, cross-entropy). Collapsed to one head in S11 (blocks retired).
